original_hypothesis.title: Nonce wraparound at uint256 max enables replay of entire nonce space (theoretical but protocol-breaking if reachable)
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Nonce._consumeNonce increments nonce in an unchecked block: newNonce = _nonce + 1. If nonce reaches type(uint256).max, it wraps to 0, allowing reuse of historical nonces and replay of previously signed payloads in that space.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a direct invariant violation described in Phase A (INV-STATE-003). Even if reaching uint256 max is unlikely, some systems use multiple spaces and automated operations; long-lived wallets could theoretically approach high nonces in some spaces, and correctness demands explicit prevention.

proof_of_signal.pattern: unchecked increment of nonce without max-bound guard

preconditions: A nonce space reaches nonce == type(uint256).max
Attacker possesses or can obtain a previously valid signature for a historical nonce in that space (on-chain observable signatures are common)
Wallet has not changed config in a way that invalidates the historical signature (or signature uses static signature/subdigest paths)

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: unchecked arithmetic wraparound on monotonic counter

analysis_questions: Are there any practical caps on nonce size via packed payload encoding (nonceSize bits) that limit maximum reachable nonce?
Can an attacker force nonce increases via any path (e.g., auth bypass) or only via valid signatures?
Do session/non-session flows share nonce spaces and could a high-throughput session reach max faster?
validation_requirements: Confirm encoding allows large nonce values (Payload.fromPackedCalls nonceSize up to 7 bytes unless extended elsewhere)
Assess whether nonce can ever approach max in production; if not, document as theoretical correctness risk
Add property-based test asserting revert when nonce == max (if patched)

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: If a wallet ever reaches nonce max in a space, the next successful execution wraps nonce to 0. Attacker replays an old signed payload from early wallet history (nonce=0) to drain funds or re-execute approvals/transfers.
prerequisites: Extremely long-lived/high-throughput wallet usage in one nonce space

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Total loss potential via arbitrary replay of previously authorized calls
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: Any wallet that can reach nonce wrap in any space
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Difficult; relies on moving funds away before replay or changing signer set in advance

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No edge-case test for nonce == type(uint256).max

technical_investigation_focus.execution_timing: multi-year accumulation; then single replay tx
technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: monitor nonce approaching max (unlikely) and flag risk

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
